methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $14.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Figure AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $39B

data.narrative:
Skild AI, a Series C-stage AI & Software company founded by Deepak Pathak and Abhinav Gupta, is evaluated against a universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir, PLTR). The target's last post-money valuation stands at $14.0B following $1.4B+ in total capital raised.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, anchoring to the $14.0B Series C valuation and applying a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier to next stage, implies a valuation of $23.8B. The peer-median method references four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: Figure AI ($39.0B Series C), Wayve ($8.6B Series D), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B Series B), and Black Forest Labs ($3.2B Series B). The median across this 4-company set is $7.1B, with a range spanning $3.2B to $39.0B.

The triangulated analysis yields a low estimate of $6.0B, a median of $15.4B, and a high estimate of $27.4B. The wide spread reflects material dispersion within the comparable set—particularly Figure AI's $39.0B outlier position—and divergence between peer medians ($7.1B) and stage-progression methodology ($23.8B). Key uncertainties include the timing and probability of next-stage fundraising, capital intensity requirements for the underlying technology, and the relevance of Figure AI's valuation premium to Skild's specific technology and commercial trajectory.
